stem: A pupil wants to count how many vowels are in a string entered by the user.
model_answer:
text = input("Enter a sentence: ").lower()
vowels = "aeiou"
count = 0
for ch in text:
    if ch in vowels:
        count = count + 1
print(count)
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: case handling (lower-case the input or compare to both cases), iteration over each character, membership test against the vowel set, counter, output.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write a program that counts the vowels in a sentence the user types. Treat upper-case and lower-case vowels the same.

mark_points.text: input read and case normalised (lower() OR explicit upper-case checks)
tests if character is a vowel and increments a counter
